items.title: Show HN: Sorted Receipts - clients dump receipts in one link, LLM sorts them
items.why_it_matters: A solo developer built and shipped Sorted Receipts, a simple tool that lets clients dump receipts into a single link for LLM sorting, capitalizing on Xero retiring Hubdoc. It is a classic example of shipping a highly practical utility using a simple backend (FastAPI + SQLite). You can study their clean, no-auth upload flow and try building a similar public 'one-click drop' link for AI-ingest this week to let users quickly submit news sources without signing up.

items.title: Show HN: Claudexor – quota-aware routing for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor
items.why_it_matters: A developer launched Claudexor, an open-source, quota-aware router for Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor that switches subscriptions on the fly to prevent hitting limits and save on token overspend. Since you actively use both Claude Code and Codex, this directly solves your daily quota headaches. You can clone the repository and set it up as a plugin in your Claude Code terminal today to manage your active sessions without hitting walls.

items.title: 40–90% fewer tokens on Claude Code via TokenOptimization
items.why_it_matters: The TokenOptimization repository was released, offering a compression tool that slashes Claude Code token usage by 40% to 90%. This directly addresses the high token costs of running agentic loops on your local files. You can run their compression script on your AI-ingest project directory before your next coding session today to instantly speed up Claude's response times and lower your daily API bill.

items.title: Show HN: Tokenmaxx – CLI that merges usage across Claude Code and Codex accounts
items.why_it_matters: A developer shipped Tokenmaxx, a CLI tool designed to merge and monitor your API usage across both Claude Code and Codex accounts. Since these are your two primary coding environments, this gives you a single dashboard for your development spend. You can install the CLI from GitHub this week to track your combined daily token costs directly from your terminal.

items.title: Show HN: Hoop – A sandboxed P2P live collaboration harness for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: Hoop is a new sandboxed, peer-to-peer live collaboration harness built specifically for Claude Code that lets you securely share your terminal-based AI sessions with others in real-time. You can install Hoop today and use it to share a live session with a friend or mentor the next time you need to debug a tricky Next.js or Supabase issue.

items.title: Show HN: CLI tool that packages data-heavy projects into LLM context windows
items.why_it_matters: The data2prompt CLI tool was released to help package data-heavy local projects and database schemas cleanly into LLM context windows. This makes it much easier to feed structured project states to Claude without hitting formatting errors. You can run this CLI on your AI-ingest directory this week to generate a perfectly formatted context file for your next feature prompt.

items.title: Show HN: Chalk: a Mac app for technical diagrams with a built-in MCP for Claude
items.why_it_matters: Chalk, a Mac app for creating technical diagrams, launched with a built-in MCP server that lets Claude edit and generate diagrams directly. This is a practical way to visualize your database and application flows using your existing Claude setup. You can download Chalk and connect its MCP server to Claude Code this week to automatically map out your Supabase schema visually.
